_description: StackOverflow data dump → ArcadeDB graph import configuration

_format_reference.vertices[]: Each entry defines a vertex type, its source file, and how to map properties and edges
_format_reference.type: ArcadeDB vertex type name (auto-created if missing)
_format_reference.file: Source file path, relative to the data directory. Format auto-detected: .xml, .csv, .jsonl
_format_reference.id: Source attribute used as integer primary key for edge resolution between types
_format_reference.nameId: Optional string-based secondary key (used by 'split' edges to resolve values by name)
_format_reference.filter: Row filter: 'attribute=value'. Only matching rows are imported. Enables splitting one file into multiple vertex types
_format_reference.element: For XML: element name to read (default 'row'). Non-'row' values auto-enable child element reading
_format_reference.properties{}: Maps ArcadeDB property names to source attributes. Format: 'dbName': 'SourceAttr' | 'int:Attr' | 'bool:Attr'
_format_reference.edges[]: Edges derived from foreign key attributes in this vertex's source file
_format_reference.edges[].attribute: Source attribute containing the foreign key value
_format_reference.edges[].edge: ArcadeDB edge type name (auto-created if missing)
_format_reference.edges[].target: Target vertex type the foreign key references
_format_reference.edges[].direction: 'out' (default): this vertex → target. 'in': target → this vertex
_format_reference.edges[].split: Delimiter for multi-value fields (e.g. '|'). One edge per value, resolved by target's nameId
_format_reference.edgeSources[]: Edge-only sources: no vertices created, both endpoints must already exist
_format_reference.edgeSources.from: Compact 'attribute:vertexType' — source attribute and its vertex type
_format_reference.edgeSources.to: Compact 'attribute:vertexType' — target attribute and its vertex type
_format_reference.limit: Optional: max records per source (for testing). Omit or 0 for unlimited

vertices._: Tags: small lookup table. 'nameId' enables split-field resolution from Question.Tags
Users: referenced by Questions, Answers, Comments, and Badges via foreign keys
Questions (PostTypeId=1). Filtered from Posts.xml. Must appear before Answer for edge resolution

edges._: User authored this question (edge: User → Question)
Tags field '|java|python|' split into one edge per tag, resolved by Tag.nameId

vertices._: Answers (PostTypeId=2). Filtered from Posts.xml. References Question via ParentId

edges._: User authored this answer (edge: User → Answer)
This answer belongs to a question (edge: Question → Answer)

vertices._: Comments on questions or answers. Separate edge types for Question vs Answer targets

edges._: User wrote this comment (edge: User → Comment)
User earned this badge (edge: User → Badge)

edgeSources._: Question accepted an answer (edge: Question → Answer). Runs as edge source because Answer vertices must exist first
Post-to-post links. Both endpoints can be Questions (most common for duplicate/related links)
